message:
fix(tty): handle POLLNVAL from poll(2) on macOS /dev/tty

On macOS, poll(2) on /dev/tty always returns POLLNVAL (0x20) even
though the fd is valid for open(), fcntl(), and nonblocking read().
After commit 7ed898a0 changed poll_tty() from count-based to
revents-based checking, POLLNVAL was not in the POLLIN|POLLERR|POLLHUP
set, so drain_available_bytes() was never called — permanently breaking
all keyboard and mouse input on macOS.

When POLLNVAL is detected and the reader is in nonblocking mode, fall
back to drain_available_bytes() directly (nonblocking read loop), with
an 8ms backoff sleep to prevent CPU spin. This restores full
interactivity on macOS with low idle CPU (~0-2%).

Closes #61

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
